label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which option is NOT a reason why the English language spread to numerous parts of the world. The original passage mentions a few things. First, the text says, "The English language was first introduced to the Americas by British colonization, beginning in 1607... Similarly, the language spread to numerous other parts of the world as a result of British trade and colonization elsewhere and the spread of the former British Empire, which, by 1921, held sway over a population of 470-570 million people..." So the reasons given are British colonization, British trade, and the spread of the former British Empire. The options are (A) British colonization, (B) the spread of the former English Empire, (C) British trade around the world, and (D) a large British population. The passage doesn't mention a large British population as a reason for the spread. It does mention the size of the population under the British Empire, but
